title: Conjecture 1 (Nadjafi-Arani, Khodashenas, and Ashrafi, 2012)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected graph and let $B_1, \ldots, B_k$ be all its non-complete blocks of respective orders $n_1, \ldots, n_k$. Then $$\eta(G) \geq \sum_{i=1}^{k}(2n_i - 6).$$
context_text: Nadjafi-Arani et al. investigated properties of $\eta(G) = \mathrm{Sz}(G) - W(G)$ and proposed this lower bound in terms of the non-complete blocks of a graph. The conjecture generalises the 2-connected case and is the central result the paper sets out to prove.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: first Theorem 2 establishes $\eta(G) \geq 2n-6$ for every 2-connected non-complete graph on $n$ vertices, and Corollary 3 then derives the general block-sum bound as a direct consequence.
paper_title: On the difference between the Szeged and Wiener index

title: Conjecture 5
statement_text: Let $G$ be a 2-connected graph of order $n \geq 10$ not isomorphic to $K_n$, $K^2_n$, or $K^{n-2}_n$. Then $\eta(G) \geq 2n$.
context_text: The authors note that while $\eta(K_n)=0$ and $\eta(K^2_n)=\eta(K^{n-2}_n)=2n-6$, computer search found only finitely many 2-connected graphs with $\eta(G)<2n$ and none on 10 or more vertices, motivating this stronger bound beyond the three exceptional families.
paper_title: On the difference between the Szeged and Wiener index
